str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - self-taught-developers
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Overcoming the Challenges of Being a Self-Taught Developer
entry.summary: In today's rapidly evolving tech industry, self-taught developers have become increasingly common. However, learning to code on one's own can be challenging due to the lack of structure, resources, feedback, and motivation. In this blog post, we explore ten strategies that aspiring and current self-taught developers can use to overcome these challenges. We provide practical tips on creating a roadmap for learning, making use of free resources, seeking feedback from peers, managing time effectively, keeping up with technology trends, balancing theory and practical application, dealing with imposter syndrome, managing burnout, and seeking mentorship and guidance. By implementing these strategies, self-taught developers can achieve success in the industry while enjoying the flexibility and freedom of learning at their own pace. We encourage our readers to share this blog post with their networks and help others overcome the challenges they may face as self-taught developers. Together, let's build a stronger community of self-taught developers!
